explanations.3: This tells PHP to enforce strict types, meaning you must pass exactly the right data type to functions or PHP will error instead of trying to convert.
explanations.17: This creates a connection string (DSN) for Oracle database using string interpolation — the curly braces inject the variable values into the string.
explanations.19: This is a type hint comment telling developers and tools that oci_connect() returns either a resource (a database connection handle) or false if it fails.
explanations.20: The empty string at the end is the character set parameter; leaving it empty tells Oracle to use the database's default character set.
explanations.38: oci_bind_by_name() links the :max_rows placeholder in the SQL to the $numRows variable, and SQLT_INT tells Oracle to treat it as an integer.
explanations.50: OCI_ASSOC + OCI_RETURN_NULLS means: return results as an associative array (keys are column names) and keep NULL values instead of converting them to empty strings.
explanations.51: The (int) cast forces $row['EMPLOYEE_ID'] to be an integer; this is defensive programming in case Oracle returns it as a string.
explanations.61: is_resource() checks if $statement is still a valid resource before trying to free it, preventing errors if it was never created.
